All edits stick to the facts you provided; any “⚑” notes mark where you might consider expanding or adding safely if you have the experience.

1. SUMMARY  
Current  
“Full-stack engineer specializing in scalable data pipelines, platforms, and backend services. Skilled in React, Python, and Java, with strong database and API expertise. Passionate about turning complex data into reliable, user-focused applications.”  

Revised  
“Engineering Specialist in IT systems and developer experience. Expert at designing and maintaining CI/CD pipelines, scalable data-processing systems and RESTful APIs using Python (Django, FastAPI). Built containerized deployments and internal dashboards for real-time monitoring and observability. Passionate about improving system reliability and developer workflows.”  

2. SKILLS  
– Group and order by relevance to the target role:  
  • Programming & Scripting: Python, Java, JavaScript, SQL  
  • Frameworks & APIs: Django, FastAPI, React, Node.js  
  • DevOps & CI/CD: CI/CD pipelines, Containerized deployments (Docker), AWS EC2  
  • Observability & Monitoring: Internal dashboards, Operational metrics ⚑ (Consider adding specific tools, e.g. Prometheus, Grafana, if you have experience)  
  • Data & Pipelines: PostgreSQL, MySQL, MongoDB, Pandas  

3. EXPERIENCE  
Software Engineering Intern, BitGo (Remote) | May 2024 – Jun 2025  
– Designed and maintained backend services and RESTful APIs in Python/Django supporting secure data flows across internal financial IT systems.  
– Collaborated on system architecture and scalable servi

response_full:
Below is a section-by-section set of recommendations and concrete line edits to help pivot your resume toward a Product Designer role. All edits stay strictly truthful to your existing experience. Gap areas are flagged with “(Gap)” and we suggest safe ways to address them.

––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––  
1. SUMMARY  
Current  
“Full-stack engineer specializing in scalable data pipelines, platforms, and backend services. Skilled in React, Python, and Java, with strong database and API expertise. Passionate about turning complex data into reliable, user-focused applications.”  

Recommendation  
– Reframe to foreground design, user empathy, and AI-powered interfaces.  
– Mention prototype or interactive UI work you’ve done.  
– Tie in client focus and accessibility awareness.  

Rewritten  
“Full-stack engineer turned product-focused designer, experienced in crafting interactive, data-driven dashboards and AI-powered features. Skilled in React and Python to prototype and deliver client-facing interfaces. Collaborates cross-functionally to balance usability, accessibility standards, and business goals.”  

––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––  
2. SKILLS  
– Reorder into 3 categories: Design & Prototyping, AI & Data, Core Technologies.  
– Highlight UI-focused tools you have.  
– Flag missing key design tools (Gap).  

Reordered Skills  
Design & Prototyping  
 • React (UI component development)  
 • HTML/CSS (responsive layouts)  
 • (Consider adding: Figma, Sketch, Adobe XD for wireframing & prototyping) (Gap)  

AI & Data  
 • PyTorch, TensorFlow Lite  
 • Pandas, NumPy  

Core Technologies  
 • Languages: JavaScript, Python, Java, SQL  
 • Frameworks: Django, FastAPI, Node.js  
 • Databases: PostgreSQL, MySQL, MongoDB  

––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––  
3. EXPERIENCE  
Emphasize UI/UX and client/user impact. Where possible, swap engineering-heavy language for design-oriented phrasing.  

Role: Software Engineering Intern, BitGo (Remote)  
May 2024 – Jun 2025  

• Before: “Designed and maintained backend services and REST APIs in Python/Django supporting secure data flows…”  
  After: “Built and iterated backend services (Python/Django) to power secure user interfaces—ensuring data integrity and fast API responses for client-facing dashboards.”  

• Before: “Collaborated with senior engineers on system design, requirements, and architecture…”  
  After: “Partnered with product and engineering teams to translate requirements into interactive UI prototypes and scalable service designs that balanced function and usability.”  

• Before: “Built internal dashboards using React + Django APIs, enabling real-time visibility into backend data workflows and operational metrics.”  
  After: “Developed React-based dashboard interfaces for internal stakeholders, focusing on clarity of data visualization, responsive layouts, and initial usability testing.”  

• Before: “Optimized Python data-processing pipelines and PostgreSQL queries, improving system scalability and reducing latency by ~40%.”  
  After: “Refined data pipelines and queries (-40% latency), enabling smoother real-time updates in user dashboards and improving overall user experience.”  

––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––  
4. PROJECTS  
Showcase any UI/UX thinking, prototyping steps, and client/user focus.  

Project: Centi – Personal Finance Web App  
• Before: “Built FastAPI backend services deployed in production, integrating financial data via ETL pipelines…”  
  After: “Architected FastAPI backend for real-time personal finance analytics, then designed and implemented React dashboards to present account insights with clear visual hierarchy and intuitive navigation.”  

Project: SentimentTrader – Real-Time Stock Sentiment Pipeline  
• Before: “Designed and implemented a full ML-driven data pipeline for real-time sentiment analytics…”  
  After: “Engineered an ML pipeline (FinBERT) for live stock sentiment and co-designed interactive UI mockups demonstrating sentiment trends—supporting client demos with clickable prototypes.” (Gap: add link to prototype or screenshots if available)  

––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––  
5. GAP PRIORITIES & SAFE IMPROVEMENTS  
1) Figma/Prototyping tools (Gap)  
   • Action: Complete a brief Figma–or Sketch–tutorial and note it under Tools or in a “Training” subsection.  

2) Accessibility (Gap)  
   • Action: Add a bullet about following WCAG guidelines or conducting basic accessibility audits on your dashboards.  

3) Client/User Research (Gap)  
   • Action: If you conducted stakeholder interviews or usability tests, add a line like “Ran 5+ user feedback sessions to refine UI flows.”  

4) Sales/Business Impact (Gap)  
   • Action: Quantify how your dashboards led to X% faster decision-making or reduced support tickets. Even internal metrics help.  

––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––  
By reframing your engineering work in terms of UI design, prototyping, and user impact—and by filling key gaps around Figma, accessibility, and client research—you’ll present a resume that speaks directly to the Product Designer responsibilities at Viget.
